_meta.description: English (source language) - Master string file for UI translations

page.title: Performance Problem Simulator - ASP.NET 4.8 on Windows - Dashboard
header.title: Performance Problem Simulator - ASP.NET 4.8
sim.title: Simulation Controls
sim.cpu.title: CPU Stress
sim.cpu.tooltip: Spawns worker threads running intensive cryptographic operations to stress CPU cores
sim.cpu.desc: Spawns worker threads running intensive cryptographic operations to stress CPU cores. Higher intensity uses more parallel threads.
sim.memory.title: Memory Pressure
sim.memory.tooltip: Allocates pinned memory blocks filled with random data to increase working set
sim.memory.desc: Allocates pinned memory blocks filled with random data. Multiple allocations stack - mimics gradual memory leak.
sim.thread.title: Thread Pool Starvation
sim.thread.tooltip: Blocks worker threads to simulate thread pool exhaustion
sim.thread.desc: Blocks worker threads using sync-over-async anti-pattern to simulate thread pool exhaustion. Server becomes unresponsive to new requests.
sim.thread.durationNoteText: .NET thread pool starts with ~CPU cores (e.g., 4-8) and grows at only 1-2 threads/second when starved. Impact is visible almost immediately since the initial pool is small.
sim.slow.title: Slow Requests
sim.slow.tooltip: Generates slow requests ideal for CLR Profiler diagnosis
sim.slow.desc: Generates requests using sync-over-async patterns that intentionally take a long time. Ideal for CLR Profiler diagnosis in Azure.
sim.slow.timeoutWarning: Note: Requests exceeding 30 seconds total time (queue + execution) are flagged as critical
sim.slow.execTimeHelp: This value controls only the processing time. Total Duration = Queue Time + Execution Time.
sim.failed.title: Failed Requests
sim.failed.tooltip: Generates HTTP 500 errors visible in AppLens and Application Insights
sim.failed.desc: Generates HTTP 5xx errors by calling the load test endpoint with 100% failure rate. Creates authentic server errors for testing error monitoring.
sim.failed.infoNoteText: Azure Portal → AppLens, Application Insights → Failures blade
sim.crash.title: Application Crash
sim.crash.tooltip: Triggers various types of fatal crashes for testing crash monitoring
sim.crash.desc: Triggers a fatal crash to test Azure crash monitoring and diagnostics. Choose from various crash types including stack overflow and access violations.
metrics.cpu.label: CPU Usage
metrics.cpu.tooltip: System CPU usage percentage across all cores
metrics.memory.label: Memory Working Set
metrics.memory.tooltip: Physical memory currently used by the process
metrics.threads.label: Thread Pool
metrics.threads.tooltip: Active worker threads in the .NET thread pool
metrics.queue.label: Queue Length
metrics.queue.tooltip: Pending work items waiting for thread pool execution
latency.title: Request Latency Monitor
latency.desc: Measures response time to a lightweight probe endpoint. Times include both processing and queue time. During thread pool starvation, latency increases dramatically.
latency.suspended: Latency probes reduced during Slow Request testing to ensure clean CLR Profile diagnostics.
activeSims.title: Active Simulations
eventLog.title: Event Log
footer.tagline: Performance Problem Simulator — Educational Tool for Azure App Service Diagnostics
log.system.runningLocal: Application is currently running on Local
log.system.runningSku: Application is currently running on {sku} SKU on worker {worker}

log.idle.goingIdle: Application going idle, no health probes being sent. There will be gaps in diagnostics and logs.
log.idle.wakingUp: App waking up from idle state. There may be gaps in diagnostics and logs.
log.crash.triggeringDelayed: CRASH: {type} in {delay}s - Connection will be lost!
log.crash.connectionLost: Application crashed! Connection lost. Waiting for restart...
log.crash.restarted: APPLICATION RESTARTED! Process ID changed from {oldPid} to {newPid}. This may indicate an unexpected crash (OOM, StackOverflow, etc.)
log.crash.confirmTitle: ⚠️ WARNING: This will CRASH the application!
log.crash.confirmBody:
The application will terminate and Azure will auto-restart it.
✓ Azure Crash Monitoring will capture this crash.

Are you sure you want to proceed?
log.slow.starting: Starting slow request simulator: {duration}s requests, {interval}s interval, max {max}
